chunks.section_scope: RFC 8999 Section 5.1 long-header invariants
chunks.suggested_dependency_notes: Foundation slice. It anchors the version-independent long-header layout used by the existing parser tests.
notes: Direct overlap with the current long-header parser and header tests.

chunks.section_scope: RFC 9000 Sections 2-2.4 stream abstractions, stream IDs, and STREAM-frame semantics
chunks.suggested_dependency_notes: Foundation slice for stream identity and stream payload parsing. It can move alongside the packet-header branch because it owns different code paths.
notes: Direct overlap with the existing stream-id and STREAM-frame parser/tests.

chunks.section_scope: RFC 9000 Sections 3-3.5 stream state machines
chunks.suggested_dependency_notes: Depends on the stream abstraction slice and on flow-control decisions from Section 4.

chunks.section_scope: RFC 9000 Sections 4-4.6 stream operations and flow control
chunks.suggested_dependency_notes: Build after the stream abstraction and state-machine chunks because the operation surface depends on both.

chunks.section_scope: RFC 9000 Sections 5-5.2.3 connection IDs, packet classification, and Version Negotiation entry points
chunks.suggested_dependency_notes: Should follow the packet-header foundation because the semantics assume long-header parsing and version visibility.
notes: Partial overlap with the existing packet-header and version-negotiation parser/tests; CID policy remains greenfield.

chunks.section_scope: RFC 9000 Sections 5.3-7 connection establishment, version negotiation, and TLS handshake integration
chunks.suggested_dependency_notes: Depends on connection identity, addressability, and packet-header primitives from earlier chunks.
notes: Greenfield connection-establishment and TLS coordination work.

chunks.section_scope: RFC 9000 Section 8 address validation and anti-amplification
chunks.suggested_dependency_notes: Depends on the connection-establishment chunk and on the packet-size primitives used to enforce amplification limits.

chunks.section_scope: RFC 9000 Sections 9-9.3.3 connection migration initiation and path validation
chunks.suggested_dependency_notes: Depends on address validation and the migration-aware connection model from the earlier connection chunks.

chunks.section_scope: RFC 9000 Sections 9.4-9.7 migrated-path accounting and preferred-address handling
chunks.suggested_dependency_notes: Depends on the path-validation and migration state established in the previous migration chunk.
notes: Greenfield migration accounting and preferred-address logic.

chunks.section_scope: RFC 9000 Sections 10-10.2.3 idle timeout, closing, and draining
chunks.suggested_dependency_notes: Should follow the migration and packet-liveness slices because the closing behavior depends on PTO and validated-path handling.

chunks.section_scope: RFC 9000 Sections 10.3-10.3.3 stateless reset generation and validation
chunks.suggested_dependency_notes: Depends on the packet-header and connection-ID primitives that make stateless reset detection possible.

chunks.section_scope: RFC 9000 Sections 11-12.5 error signaling and packet-protection rules
chunks.suggested_dependency_notes: Build after the packet-header foundation because the error and protection rules sit on the same packet model.

chunks.section_scope: RFC 9000 Sections 13-13.2.7 packet acknowledgment processing
chunks.suggested_dependency_notes: Prepares the ACK bookkeeping needed by RFC 9002; should land before any recovery work.

chunks.section_scope: RFC 9000 Sections 13.3-13.4.2.2 retransmission loss signaling and ECN validation
chunks.suggested_dependency_notes: Depends on the ACK-processing chunk and feeds directly into loss-recovery logic.

chunks.section_scope: RFC 9000 Section 14 datagram sizing and PMTU discovery
chunks.suggested_dependency_notes: Depends on packet-format plumbing and on the packet/ACK model that already exists by this point.
notes: Greenfield datagram sizing and PMTU discovery.

chunks.section_scope: RFC 9000 Sections 15-17.2 version numbers, varints, and packet-header wire format
chunks.suggested_dependency_notes: Foundation slice for packet-wire encoding; it should be available before the rest of the header and frame chunks.
notes: Direct overlap with the existing varint parser and packet-header tests.

chunks.section_scope: RFC 9000 Sections 17.2.1-17.2.2 Version Negotiation and Initial packet headers
chunks.suggested_dependency_notes: Depends on the packet-header foundation and is the next step after the version/varint chunk.
notes: Direct overlap with the existing long-header and version-negotiation tests.

chunks.section_scope: RFC 9000 Sections 17.2.3-17.2.5.3 0-RTT, Handshake, and Retry packet types
chunks.suggested_dependency_notes: Depends on the packet-header foundation and the Version Negotiation / Initial packet chunk.
notes: Partial overlap with long-header parsing tests; 0-RTT, Handshake, and Retry semantics are greenfield.

chunks.section_scope: RFC 9000 Sections 17.3-17.4 short-header packet type and spin-bit observability
chunks.suggested_dependency_notes: Depends on the packet-header foundation, but the short-header parser slice already exists in the repo.
notes: Direct overlap with the existing short-header parser/tests; spin-bit observability is still greenfield.

chunks.section_scope: RFC 9000 Sections 18-18.2 transport parameter encoding
chunks.suggested_dependency_notes: Depends on the packet and TLS plumbing, but the transport-parameter encoder itself is a clean standalone slice.

chunks.section_scope: RFC 9000 Sections 19.1-19.3.2 PADDING, PING, and ACK frame formats
chunks.suggested_dependency_notes: Build after packet-protection and ACK-processing primitives are in place.

chunks.section_scope: RFC 9000 Sections 19.4-19.8 RESET_STREAM, STOP_SENDING, CRYPTO, and STREAM frame formats
chunks.suggested_dependency_notes: Depends on the shared frame parser surface and on the stream parser module already present in the repo.
notes: Direct overlap with the existing STREAM-frame parser/tests; the non-STREAM frame types remain greenfield.

chunks.section_scope: RFC 9000 Sections 19.9-19.14 flow-control frame formats
chunks.suggested_dependency_notes: Should follow the ACK and packet bookkeeping chunks because these frames are tied to flow-control state.

chunks.section_scope: RFC 9000 Sections 19.15-19.18 connection-ID and path-probe frames
chunks.suggested_dependency_notes: Depends on the migration and connection-ID chunks because these frames are part of that control plane.
notes: Greenfield connection-ID and path-probe frame work.

chunks.section_scope: RFC 9000 Sections 19.19-19.21 connection close, handshake done, and frame encoding rules
chunks.suggested_dependency_notes: Depends on the error-handling and packet-protection chunks because these behaviors sit on the shutdown path.

chunks.section_scope: RFC 9000 Sections 20-21.12 transport error codes and security considerations
chunks.suggested_dependency_notes: Depends on the packet and connection-lifecycle behavior from earlier chunks, but can be implemented as a mostly documentation/policy track.

chunks.section_scope: RFC 9000 Section 22 IANA registry rules and codepoints
chunks.suggested_dependency_notes: Registry policy can run after the frame and transport-codepoint surfaces are stable.

chunks.section_scope: RFC 9001 Sections 2-5 TLS packet protection, CRYPTO delivery, and key derivation
chunks.suggested_dependency_notes: Depends on the packet-header and stream/frame plumbing from RFC 9000, especially the packet-number and CRYPTO handling slices.

chunks.section_scope: RFC 9001 Sections 6-10 and Appendix B key update, security considerations, and AEAD limits
chunks.suggested_dependency_notes: Depends on the packet-protection chunk and the packet-header / packet-number primitives from RFC 9000.

chunks.section_scope: RFC 9002 Sections 2-5.3 ack-eliciting packets, packets in flight, and RTT estimation
chunks.suggested_dependency_notes: Depends on packet accounting and ACK-processing from RFC 9000.

chunks.section_scope: RFC 9002 Sections 6-6.4 loss detection and PTO scheduling
chunks.suggested_dependency_notes: Depends on the RTT-estimation chunk and on the packet-number-space model from RFC 9000.

chunks.section_scope: RFC 9002 Sections 7-7.8 congestion control, ECN, and persistent congestion
chunks.suggested_dependency_notes: Depends on the loss-detection chunk and on the ACK/loss bookkeeping that precedes it.

chunks.section_scope: RFC 9002 Appendix A pseudocode and recovery bookkeeping
chunks.suggested_dependency_notes: Build after the core RTT, loss, and congestion-control chunks because Appendix A mirrors those algorithms in executable form.
notes: Greenfield appendix pseudocode and state-bookkeeping work.

chunks.section_scope: RFC 9002 Appendix B congestion-control constants and window updates
chunks.suggested_dependency_notes: Build after the core congestion-control chunk because Appendix B formalizes the window-update rules.

parallelization_plan.rationale: Own the packet-header and version/varint foundation in one branch so the long-header parser, version-negotiation logic, and short-header parser can move together without merge churn.
Own the stream-ID and STREAM-frame surface in a separate branch from the packet-header work; this keeps the existing stream parser/test slice isolated.
Run the remaining RFC 9000 transport slices sequentially in one worktree once the foundation branch lands; most of these chunks touch shared transport state or frame-handling code.
Keep the RFC 9001 packet-protection work isolated from the transport core so the TLS-specific code can be reviewed on its own after the packet-format primitives stabilize.
Run RFC 9002 as its own recovery branch after the ACK and packet-accounting slices are stable; the sections are algorithmically dependent and should stay sequential within one worktree.
